item.text:
**Summary of work (Stage 1 — creation)**

- I created a small deterministic Dinosaur Jump prototype under `game/` and supporting files in the stage root.
- Added deterministic hooks:
  - `window.advanceTime(ms)` — advances the game state by `ms` milliseconds.
  - `window.render_game_to_text()` — returns a one-line textual snapshot of the game state.
- Implemented:
  - One obstacle type (cactus).
  - Distance score (derived from time * speed).
  - `game_over` state when collision occurs.
- Added a small static server `serve.js` that serves `game/dist`.
- Added Playwright-style tests at `tests/playwright.spec.ts`. I attempted to install Playwright but the environment has npm permission errors, so I added a Node-based test harness that exercises the same deterministic hooks:
  - `tests/node_tests.js` — uses Node `vm` to load `game/dist/main.js` and runs two tests:
    - Score increases after advancing 10,000 ms.
    - Forced collision sets `game_over`.
  - Running `node tests/node_tests.js` in this environment passed (output shows both tests pass).

Files added (important ones)
- `game/src/main.ts` — TypeScript source (deterministic game logic and hooks).
- `game/dist/main.js` — Compiled JS used for tests / simple serving.
- `game/dist/index.html` — Simple HTML to load `main.js`.
- `serve.js` — Simple static file server (serves `game/dist` on port 3000).
- `tests/playwright.spec.ts` — Playwright-style tests (require Playwright to run).
- `tests/node_tests.js` — Node-based deterministic tests (works without Playwright).
- `progress.md` — short progress log.

What I ran and results
- Created the project files and server.
- Attempted `npm i @playwright/test` but npm failed due to permissions (EACCES /root/.npmrc). Because installing Playwright failed, I implemented `tests/node_tests.js` and executed it; it passed:

Example run output (from the environment)
- score text: P@80,0 | obstacles=700:20x40 | score=1200 | game_over=false
- score test passed -> 1200
- post-collision text: P@80,0 | obstacles=699:20x40,89:40x40,700:20x40 | score=1201 | game_over=true
- collision test passed
- ALL OK

How to run locally (on your machine)
- If you want to run the Playwright tests:
  1. Ensure Node.js and npm are installed.
  2. From the stage root run:
     - npm install
     - npm i --save-dev @playwright/test
     - npx playwright install
     - npm test
  3. Or run the Playwright tests directly with `npx playwright test --workers=1`.
- To run the included Node-based deterministic tests (no Playwright required):
  1. From the stage root run: `node tests/node_tests.js`
  2. To serve the built game in a browser: `node serve.js` then open `http://127.0.0.1:3000`.

Notes and constraints
- I kept all changes restricted to this stage folder as requested.
- The environment here had npm permission restrictions so I could not install Playwright to execute the real browser tests. To keep progress moving, I provided:
  - Playwright-style tests (`tests/playwright.spec.ts`) so you can run them locally after installing Playwright.
  - A Node-based test (`tests/node_tests.js`) that validates the deterministic hooks and passes in this environment.
- Implementation is intentionally small and readable; `game/dist/main.js` is the runnable JS that exposes the two required hooks.

If you want next steps, I can:
- Try to fix the npm permission issue (requires environment permission changes).
- Install Playwright and run the browser tests (on your approval / if you provide permission).
- Expand the game (graphics, jump input, more obstacle types).
- Convert `game/src/main.ts` into a proper Vite build (I included minimal `tsconfig` and `vite.config.ts` already).

Files you can open directly:
- `game/src/main.ts`
- `game/dist/main.js`
- `game/dist/index.html`
- `serve.js`
- `tests/playwright.spec.ts`
- `tests/node_tests.js`
- `progress.md`

Would you like me to try addressing the npm permission issue, or proceed to expand the game or add CI config to run Playwright tests remotely?
